title: Winter Wonderland art exhibit opens
description: GEORGE NEWS - The opening of the South Cape...
bodyText:
GEORGE NEWS - The opening of the South Cape Association for Visual Arts' (Scava) 'Winter Wonderland' art exhibition was extremely well supported by patrons and art lovers.

The auditorium was abuzz with excitement as guests mingled, their fingers wrapped around mugs of heart-warming soup.


> Visitors chatted to Scava artists about their styles and techniques. Scava also welcomed many new members and artists who debuted their work at the museum.


Artists and visitors were impressed with the talent of the association's artists as evident from the eclectic, absorbing artworks.

Also part of the exhibition are some delightful works of Carpe Diem School.

'Winter Wonderland' can be viewed in the Sayers auditorium at George Museum until 14 August.

For more information, contact Scava at [email protected].

Scava secretary Liezel du Toit (left) and chairperson Rene Scribante.

Enjoying the opening evening, from left: Lorinda Nel, Jorette Roos and Scava secretary Liezel du Toit.

Some of the art works on display.

The opening of Winter Wonderland drew a large crowd.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
